fidelityNote: Per the NCEES FE Reference Handbook v10.6 (Civil Eng. → Hydrology/Water Resources, p. 296): The Rational Method $Q=CIA$ (US units; note the Handbook's capital $I$) and the NRCS/SCS rainfall-runoff equations $Q=(P-0.2S)^2/(P+0.8S)$ with $S=1000/CN-10$ (inches) are printed verbatim in the Handbook. The following are standard practice but are NOT printed in v10.6 — memorize them: the SI Rational form ($/360$), the SI curve-number form $S=25400/CN-254$ (mm), the standalone $I_a=0.2S$, return period $T=1/P$, the risk formula $1-(1-1/T)^n$, and the IDF intensity equation.

questions.stem: A watershed has a drainage area of 200 acres, a runoff coefficient C = 0.65, and a design rainfall intensity of 2.5 in/hr. Using the Rational Method, the peak runoff rate is most nearly:
equations: $Q = CIA$ (USCS: $C$ dimensionless, $I$ in in/hr, $A$ in acres, $Q$ in ft³/s)

questions.stem: A 40-ha urban watershed has a runoff coefficient C = 0.55 and a design intensity of 72 mm/hr. Using the SI Rational Method, the peak runoff rate is most nearly:
equations: $Q = \dfrac{CIA}{360}$ (SI: $I$ in mm/hr, $A$ in ha, $Q$ in m³/s)

questions.stem: A watershed has a SCS Curve Number CN = 80. The maximum potential retention S (USCS) is most nearly:
A watershed with CN = 80 receives a total storm precipitation P = 4.0 in. Using the SCS method, the direct runoff depth Q is most nearly:
For a watershed with CN = 75, the SCS initial abstraction Ia (the precipitation that must fall before any runoff occurs) is most nearly:
A watershed has CN = 75 (S = 3.33 in, Ia = 0.67 in). A storm produces only P = 0.5 in of precipitation. The direct runoff depth Q from this storm is:
questions.solution: $P = 0.5\ \mathrm{in} < I_a = 0.67\ \mathrm{in}$, so the entire precipitation is captured by initial abstraction and $Q = 0$.

questions.concept: Return period to annual exceedance probability
questions.stem: The "50-year storm" has an annual exceedance probability (AEP) of:
questions.handbook: Surface Water — Frequency Analysis (fundamental — return period T = 1/P is fundamental; not a Handbook v10.6 formula)

questions.concept: Annual exceedance probability to return period
questions.stem: A design storm has an annual exceedance probability (AEP) of 1%. Its return period T is:
questions.handbook: Surface Water — Frequency Analysis (fundamental — return period T = 1/P is fundamental; not a Handbook v10.6 formula)

questions.concept: Risk: probability of at least one exceedance in n years
questions.stem: What is the probability that a 100-year storm will be equaled or exceeded at least once during a 50-year design life?
questions.handbook: Surface Water — Frequency Analysis (fundamental — risk 1−(1−1/T)ⁿ is fundamental probability; not a Handbook v10.6 formula)

questions.stem: A watershed consists of three sub-areas: 8 acres (C = 0.30), 6 acres (C = 0.70), and 6 acres (C = 0.90). The composite runoff coefficient C_comp for use in the Rational Method is most nearly:
In the USCS Rational Method equation Q = CIA, when C is dimensionless, I is in inches per hour, and A is in acres, the resulting Q has units of:
questions.solution: By the unit conversion $1\ \mathrm{in/hr} \times 1\ \mathrm{acre} = 1.0083\ \mathrm{cfs} \approx 1\ \mathrm{cfs}$, so $Q = CIA$ yields peak flow in ft³/s (cfs) without any unit-conversion factor.

questions.stem: On an Intensity–Duration–Frequency (IDF) curve, for a fixed return period (e.g., 100-year), as the storm duration increases from 5 minutes to 24 hours, the design rainfall intensity:
questions.solution: For a fixed return period, IDF curves show that intensity decreases as duration increases — a 100-year, 5-min storm is far more intense than a 100-year, 24-hr storm. This is a fundamental property of all IDF curves.

questions.stem: A watershed has CN = 75. Using the SI version of the SCS equation, the maximum potential retention S is most nearly:
A watershed with CN = 80 receives total storm precipitation P = 100 mm. Using the SI SCS method, the direct runoff depth Q is most nearly:
A 500-acre watershed with CN = 80 produces a direct runoff depth of Q = 2.04 in from a design storm. The total direct runoff volume is most nearly:
equations: $V = Q_{\text{depth}}\times A$ (convert depth in to ft: divide by 12)

questions.stem: For the same storm precipitation P, a watershed with CN = 90 will produce __________ direct runoff than one with CN = 70.
equations: $S = \dfrac{1000}{CN} - 10$; higher CN $\Rightarrow$ smaller S $\Rightarrow$ less retention
options: cannot be determined without knowing P
questions.solution: Higher CN → smaller S → smaller initial abstraction $I_a = 0.2S$ → runoff begins sooner and the denominator in the SCS equation is smaller, yielding more runoff Q for the same P.

questions.concept: Runoff coefficient C — physical meaning and range
questions.stem: In the Rational Method Q = CIA, the runoff coefficient C represents the fraction of rainfall that becomes direct runoff. Its physically valid range is:
questions.solution: C is a dimensionless ratio (runoff/rainfall); it cannot exceed 1 (no more runoff than rain) and cannot be negative. Typical values: 0.10 (woods) to 0.95 (impervious pavement).

questions.concept: Annual exceedance probability of the 25-year event
questions.stem: The annual exceedance probability (AEP) of the 25-year design storm is:
questions.handbook: Surface Water — Frequency Analysis (fundamental — return period T = 1/P is fundamental; not a Handbook v10.6 formula)

questions.stem: A 200-ha watershed has CN = 75 and receives a total storm precipitation of P = 120 mm. Using the SI SCS method, determine the total direct runoff volume (in m³).
equations: $V = Q \times A$ (convert mm to m, ha to m²)

questions.stem: A 120-acre residential watershed has a composite runoff coefficient C = 0.45 and a 10-year, 1-hour rainfall intensity of 2.8 in/hr. Using the Rational Method, find the peak discharge Q (in cfs).
